kernel: N134 PSWZ -- N107 PY 4-warp 64x64 HGEMM + 4x4 super-block CTA-swizzle
host_driver: byte-identical to N140 host_one.c (grid=(M/64,M/64) block=128 k_arg=K/16)
verdict: MECHANISM CONFIRMED -- swizzle restores L2 reuse; L2 hit 56% -> 87% @ M=6144, exceeding 80% prediction
prediction: swizzle restores L2 hit rate 56% -> 80%+ @ M=6144
ncu_replay_caveat: ncu kernel-replay wall (0.4-4.2 TFLOPS) is NOT real perf; counters are replay-invariant. Real perf = N134 result.json + this cycle nsys 20-rep.

nsys_sanity_tflops.note: matches N134 real perf (58.03/46.37/44.17) -- no replay contamination

shapes.note: control flat as expected -- validates measurement is shape-specific

causal_chain: 4x4 super-block swizzle -> concurrent CTAs share 256x256 tiles -> super-block working set ~6MB << 32MB L2 -> L2 HIT 56->87% -> DRAM bytes 3.3x less -> DRAM un-saturates (224->199 GB/s) -> warp-cycles/inst 122->43 (2.85x) -> eligible warps 0.10->0.33 (3.3x) -> compute SM 14->40% -> gpc cycles 2.82x fewer == +180% throughput
controlled_ab: kernel body byte-identical (inst_executed within 0.05%, occupancy identical ~65%); ONLY changed variable = CTA visitation order; isolates L2 reuse as sole cause
honest_caveats: L2 hit plateaus ~87% (not M=4096's 98%) -- full A+B working set still > L2; swizzle bounds the CONCURRENT super-block working set, not the whole matrix. Explains N134 ratio plateau 0.62-0.66 (not peak ~0.78) and residual gap to cuBLAS.
Occupancy unchanged (~65%, register-limited) -- cliff/recovery is a memory-latency/scheduler story, NOT occupancy.
ncu run on ubu-2 not ubu-1 (where N140 ran); same GPU model+driver, M=4096 control matches N140 within 0.03pts L2 / 3% DRAM bytes -> cross-host comparison valid.
N130's 'L2-thrash vs DRAM-bandwidth' either/or is resolved: ONE chain -- L2-thrash CAUSES DRAM-bandwidth saturation.
